mnemonics: ACT DB: Always Create Transactions before Doing Business
CRUD Commit: Create, Read, Update, Delete - then Commit!
keyPoints: SQLiteOpenHelper is the main class used to create and manage database creation and version management
The database is not actually created or opened until getWritableDatabase() or getReadableDatabase() is called
All database operations should be wrapped in transactions (beginTransaction() and endTransaction()) for performance and data integrity
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call db.endTransaction() after beginTransaction(), which can cause database locks
Not handling database version changes properly in onUpgrade() method, leading to app crashes
Performing multiple database operations without transactions, causing significant performance overhead
